name: c4-context
description: Expert C4 Context-level documentation specialist. Creates
capabilities: Generate C4 Context-level system documentation with personas and user journeys
Create Mermaid C4Context diagrams showing system boundaries and external dependencies
Document all human and programmatic users interacting with the system
Map user journeys for key features across different personas
Identify and document external systems, APIs, and service dependencies
Synthesize existing system documentation into comprehensive context architecture
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines of executable code. This is a prompt-only documentation skill containing templates and instructions for creating C4 system context diagrams. No security risks detected: no executable code, network calls, filesystem operations, or external command execution. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Software teams struggle to create clear, stakeholder-friendly architecture documentation that communicates system boundaries and external dependencies. This skill generates comprehensive C4 Context-level documentation including personas, user journeys, system features, and Mermaid diagrams.
userTitle: Create C4 System Context Architecture Documentation
limitations: Requires existing system documentation or codebase context to analyze
Does not generate C4 Container or C4 Component level diagrams
Cannot validate actual external system integrations or API contracts
Output quality depends on input information completeness

useCases.targetUser: Software architect documenting a new microservices platform
useCases.title: New System Documentation
useCases.description: Create comprehensive C4 Context documentation for a newly designed system to share with stakeholders and development teams.

useCases.targetUser: Engineering manager onboarding new developers to a legacy application
useCases.title: Legacy System Analysis
useCases.description: Generate system context documentation for an existing codebase to improve team understanding and onboarding.

useCases.targetUser: Product manager preparing architecture overview for executive review
useCases.title: Stakeholder Communication
useCases.description: Produce stakeholder-friendly architecture documentation for non-technical audiences including business leaders.

promptTemplates.title: Basic System Context
promptTemplates.prompt: Create C4 Context-level documentation for a [system type] that [primary purpose]. Include the main user personas and list the top 3 external system dependencies.

promptTemplates.title: Complete Context Documentation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate comprehensive C4 Context documentation including: short and long system descriptions, all personas (human and programmatic), complete feature list, user journey maps for each key feature, external system dependencies, and a Mermaid C4Context diagram.

promptTemplates.title: User Journey Focus
promptTemplates.prompt: For a [system name] with [number] key features, create detailed user journey maps for each persona. Show step-by-step how each persona interacts with the system to accomplish their goals. Include error handling paths.

promptTemplates.title: External Dependency Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this system's external dependencies and create comprehensive documentation. For each external system, document: type (database/API/service), integration method, data flow direction, authentication requirements, and failure impact. Generate a Mermaid diagram showing all external interactions.

bestPractices: Focus on people and software systems rather than technologies and protocols in Context diagrams
Include both human users and programmatic users in persona documentation for complete system view
Keep C4 Context diagrams stakeholder-friendly by avoiding technical implementation details
antiPatterns: Showing technologies, protocols, or infrastructure details in Context-level diagrams
Omitting programmatic users and external systems that interact with the system
Mixing C4 levels by including component or container details in Context documentation

faq.question: What is the C4 model and why use it?
faq.answer: The C4 model is a hierarchical approach to software architecture diagrams with four levels: Context, Container, Component, and Code. It helps teams communicate architecture at different levels of detail to different audiences.

faq.question: What is a System Context diagram?
faq.answer: A System Context diagram shows your system as a box in the center, surrounded by its users and the other systems it interacts with. It focuses on people and software systems rather than technical implementation details.

faq.question: Who should use C4 Context documentation?
faq.answer: System Context documentation is ideal for stakeholders, product managers, new team members, and anyone needing a high-level understanding of what the system does and how it fits into the broader ecosystem.

faq.question: What is the difference between C4 Context and C4 Container?
faq.answer: C4 Context shows the system as a single box with users and external systems. C4 Container zooms inside to show the high-level technical building blocks like web applications, databases, and microservices.

faq.question: Can this skill generate Mermaid diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill generates Mermaid C4Context diagrams using proper C4 model syntax including Person, System, System_Ext, SystemDb elements and Rel relationships.

faq.question: What input does this skill need to generate documentation?
faq.answer: Provide basic system information including purpose, target users, key features, and known external dependencies. More detailed input produces more comprehensive output documentation.
